meta.institution: SRM Institute of Science and Technology

qa_pairs.question: What is the semester fee for BTech CSE?
qa_pairs.answer: The semester fee for B.Tech CSE is ₹1,10,000 per semester.

qa_pairs.question: How much is the tuition fee?
qa_pairs.answer: The tuition fee for B.Tech CSE is ₹1,10,000 per semester, totalling approximately ₹8,80,000 for the full 4-year programme.

qa_pairs.question: What is the fee structure for BTech?
qa_pairs.answer: B.Tech CSE: ₹1,10,000/semester. ECE/EEE: ₹95,000/semester. Mechanical/Civil: ₹85,000/semester. Fees are revised annually.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total course fee?
qa_pairs.answer: The total fee for B.Tech CSE over 8 semesters is approximately ₹8,80,000 excluding hostel and other charges.

qa_pairs.question: When is the fee payment deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: Fee payment deadline is within 30 days from the start of each semester. Late payments attract a penalty of ₹500 per week.

qa_pairs.question: What is the last date to pay fees?
qa_pairs.answer: Fees must be paid within 30 days of semester commencement. Check the ACADEMIA portal for the exact date each semester.

qa_pairs.question: What are the fee payment modes?
qa_pairs.answer: Fees can be paid online via the SRM ACADEMIA portal (net banking, UPI, debit/credit card), Demand Draft, or direct bank transfer.

qa_pairs.question: How can I pay my semester fee online?
qa_pairs.answer: Log in to https://academia.srmist.edu.in, go to Finance then Fee Payment. You can pay via UPI, net banking, or card.
answer_steps: Go to https://academia.srmist.edu.in and log in.
Click on Finance in the top menu.
Select Fee Payment.
Choose your payment mode: UPI, net banking, or card.
Complete the payment and download your receipt.

qa_pairs.question: What is the late fee penalty?
qa_pairs.answer: A late fee penalty of ₹500 per week is charged after the payment deadline. Continued non-payment may result in debarment from exams.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any scholarships available?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Merit scholarships are available for students in the top 10% of the batch. Government scholarships (SC/ST/OBC) are also applicable. Apply via the scholarship portal on ACADEMIA.

qa_pairs.question: How do I apply for a scholarship?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply through ACADEMIA portal under Scholarships. Submit required documents such as mark sheets, income certificate, and community certificate. Deadlines are announced at the start of each academic year.
answer_steps: Log in to https://academia.srmist.edu.in.
Go to Scholarships under the Student menu.
Select the applicable scholarship scheme.
Upload mark sheets, income certificate, and community certificate.
Submit before the announced deadline.

qa_pairs.question: How can I get my fee receipt?
qa_pairs.answer: Fee receipts are available on the ACADEMIA portal under Finance then Fee Receipt. You can download and print them anytime.

qa_pairs.question: What is the refund policy?
qa_pairs.answer: Refund policy: 100% within 15 days of joining, 80% within 30 days, 50% within 60 days, no refund after 60 days. Contact the finance office for withdrawal procedures.

qa_pairs.question: What is the hostel fee?
qa_pairs.answer: Hostel fees range from ₹80,000 to ₹1,20,000 per year depending on room type. AC rooms cost more. Includes accommodation and basic amenities.

qa_pairs.question: What is the mess fee?
qa_pairs.answer: Mess fee is approximately ₹45,000 to ₹55,000 per year for three meals a day. Vegetarian and non-vegetarian options are available.

qa_pairs.question: What is the transport fee?
qa_pairs.answer: Transport fee varies by route and distance: ₹15,000 to ₹40,000 per year. Bus pass details are available at the transport office near the main gate.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a fee concession for economically weaker students?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, EWS students can apply for fee concession with income certificate and relevant documents at the finance office.

qa_pairs.question: What is the internal assessment pattern?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 3 Internal Assessment cycles per semester. Cycle 1 and 2 are 15 marks each, Cycle 3 is 10 marks. Best 2 out of 3 are considered, totalling 40 marks.

qa_pairs.question: How many internal exams are there?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 3 internal assessment cycles per semester. Each IA covers the syllabus taught up to that point. Best 2 scores out of 3 are counted.

qa_pairs.question: When is the end semester exam?
qa_pairs.answer: End Semester Exams are held in November for odd semesters and April for even semesters. ESE carries 60 marks out of 100.

qa_pairs.question: When are the final exams?
qa_pairs.answer: Final exams are conducted in November for odd semesters and April for even semesters. Timetable is released 2 weeks before.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get my hall ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: Hall tickets are available on ACADEMIA under Examination then Hall Ticket, one week before exams. Print and carry it on all exam days.
answer_steps: Log in to https://academia.srmist.edu.in.
Go to Examination in the top menu.
Click Hall Ticket.
Download and print your hall ticket.
Carry it on every exam day without fail.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I download my hall ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: Download your hall ticket from ACADEMIA portal under Examination then Hall Ticket. It is available 7 days before the exam.

qa_pairs.question: What is the passing criteria?
qa_pairs.answer: You need minimum 50% in the End Semester Exam (30 out of 60) AND minimum 50% aggregate (50 out of 100) to pass. Both conditions must be met.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum marks to pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimum 30 marks in ESE out of 60 and minimum 50 marks in total out of 100 is required to pass a subject.

qa_pairs.question: When are supplementary exams held?
qa_pairs.answer: Supplementary exams are held in June for odd semester failures and December for even semester failures. Register via ACADEMIA within the announced deadline.

qa_pairs.question: How can I apply for revaluation?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for revaluation within 10 days of result publication on ACADEMIA under Examination then Revaluation. Fee is ₹300 per subject.
answer_steps: Wait for the result to be published on ACADEMIA.
Go to Examination then Revaluation within 10 days of result.
Select the subject(s) for revaluation.
Pay ₹300 per subject online.
Submit and note your application number.

qa_pairs.question: What is the revaluation fee?
qa_pairs.answer: Revaluation fee is ₹300 per subject. Apply within 10 days of result declaration on the ACADEMIA portal.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the academic calendar?
qa_pairs.answer: The academic calendar is published on the official SRM website and on ACADEMIA under Academics then Academic Calendar.

qa_pairs.question: What is the grading system?
qa_pairs.answer: SRM follows a 10-point GPA system: O=10, A+=9, A=8, B+=7, B=6, C=5. Minimum CGPA of 5.0 is required to continue.

qa_pairs.question: How is CGPA calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: CGPA is the weighted average of all semester GPAs based on credits. Formula: Sum of (Grade Points x Credits) divided by Total Credits. Minimum passing CGPA is 5.0.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I fail a subject?
qa_pairs.answer: If you fail a subject, you must appear in the supplementary exam conducted in June or December. The subject is carried over until cleared.

qa_pairs.question: What is the arrear exam?
qa_pairs.answer: Arrear exams are for students who failed subjects. Held in June and December. Register on ACADEMIA within the deadline. Fee applies per subject.

qa_pairs.question: What is the attendance requirement to write exams?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimum 75% attendance is required to be eligible to write the End Semester Exam. Students below 75% are detained.

qa_pairs.question: Can I write exam with less than 75% attendance?
qa_pairs.answer: No. 75% attendance is mandatory to appear in End Semester Exams. Students with 65 to 74% may apply for condonation with a valid medical certificate.

qa_pairs.question: What are the class timings?
qa_pairs.answer: Classes run from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday. Each period is 50 minutes long.

qa_pairs.question: What time do classes start and end?
qa_pairs.answer: Classes start at 8:00 AM and end at 5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday.

qa_pairs.question: When is the lunch break?
qa_pairs.answer: Lunch break is from 12:50 PM to 1:40 PM every day.

qa_pairs.question: When is the timetable released?
qa_pairs.answer: The class timetable is released one week before semester commencement on the ACADEMIA portal under My Timetable.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find my timetable?
qa_pairs.answer: Your timetable is on ACADEMIA portal under My Timetable. It shows all subjects, faculty names, room numbers, and timings.

qa_pairs.question: What are the lab session timings?
qa_pairs.answer: Lab sessions are 3-hour continuous slots such as 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM or 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. Schedule is on ACADEMIA under My Timetable.

qa_pairs.question: Are there classes on Saturday?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Saturday is a working half-day with classes from 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM for most departments.

qa_pairs.question: When are the semester holidays?
qa_pairs.answer: Summer vacation is typically in May-June and winter break in December. Check the academic calendar on ACADEMIA for exact dates.

qa_pairs.question: How many periods are there in a day?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 8 periods per day, each 50 minutes long. Periods run from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM with lunch from 12:50 to 1:40 PM.

qa_pairs.question: When does the new semester start?
qa_pairs.answer: Odd semesters begin in July and even semesters in January. Exact dates are in the academic calendar on the SRM website.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum attendance required?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimum 75% attendance is required in each subject to be eligible for the End Semester Exam. Below 75% results in detention.

qa_pairs.question: How do I check my attendance?
qa_pairs.answer: Check attendance on ACADEMIA portal under Attendance. It shows subject-wise percentage updated daily after each class.
answer_steps: Log in to https://academia.srmist.edu.in.
Click Attendance from the top menu.
View your subject-wise attendance percentage.
Attendance is updated after each class daily.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if my attendance is below 75 percent?
qa_pairs.answer: If attendance is below 75%, you are detained and cannot appear in the End Semester Exam for that subject. You must re-register next semester.

qa_pairs.question: Can I get attendance condonation?
qa_pairs.answer: Students with 65 to 74% attendance may apply for condonation with valid medical certificates approved by the HOD. Below 65% no condonation is granted.

qa_pairs.question: How is attendance calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: Attendance = (Classes attended divided by Total classes held) x 100. Calculated separately for each subject including theory and lab.

qa_pairs.question: How do I apply for OD?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for On Duty through ACADEMIA portal under OD Application, or submit a letter to your class advisor with event details before the event date.
answer_steps: Log in to ACADEMIA and go to OD Application.
Or write a letter to your class advisor mentioning the event name, date, and purpose.
Get your HOD's approval before the event.
Submit the approved letter to the department office.
OD days will be marked as present after approval.

qa_pairs.question: What is OD attendance?
qa_pairs.answer: On Duty attendance is granted for students attending official events, placements, or inter-college competitions with prior HOD approval. OD days count as present.

qa_pairs.question: When are results published?
qa_pairs.answer: End Semester results are published 3 to 4 weeks after the last exam. Results are available on ACADEMIA under Examination then Results.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I check my results?
qa_pairs.answer: Results are on ACADEMIA at https://academia.srmist.edu.in under Examination then Results. Use your register number to view.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get my mark sheet?
qa_pairs.answer: Official mark sheets are issued by the Examination Department after each semester. Provisional mark sheets can be downloaded from ACADEMIA.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get my transcript?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for transcripts at the Examination Department with a request letter and fee of ₹500 per copy. Takes 7 to 10 working days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum CGPA to pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimum CGPA of 5.0 is required. Students below 5.0 after any semester are placed on academic probation.

qa_pairs.question: How do I improve my CGPA?
qa_pairs.answer: You can improve CGPA by re-appearing in exams for previously passed subjects via Grade Improvement. Apply via ACADEMIA during the announcement period.

qa_pairs.question: How do I apply for hostel?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for hostel through ACADEMIA under Hostel then Apply. Allotment is on a first-come-first-served basis.
answer_steps: Log in to https://academia.srmist.edu.in.
Go to Hostel under the Student menu.
Click Apply and fill in your room preference.
Pay the hostel fee online.
Wait for the allotment confirmation email.

qa_pairs.question: What are the hostel timings?
qa_pairs.answer: Hostel in-time is 9:30 PM on weekdays and 10:00 PM on weekends. Students must sign the in/out register at the hostel office.

qa_pairs.question: Is Wi-Fi available in hostel?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Wi-Fi is available in all hostels from 6:00 AM to 11:00 PM. Each student gets a login credential.

qa_pairs.question: Are there separate hostels for boys and girls?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, SRM has separate hostels for boys and girls, both within campus.

qa_pairs.question: What is the placement eligibility criteria?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimum 60% or 6.0 CGPA with no active arrears is required for most drives. Some companies require 65% or 7.0 CGPA. Check the CDC portal for each company.

qa_pairs.question: How do I register for placements?
qa_pairs.answer: Register on the SRM CDC portal at cdc.srmist.edu.in. Upload your resume, mark sheets, and complete your profile before applying to drives.
answer_steps: Go to https://cdc.srmist.edu.in and log in.
Complete your student profile with all academic details.
Upload your latest resume and mark sheets.
Browse open placement drives and apply.
Track application status from the dashboard.

qa_pairs.question: When do campus placements start?
qa_pairs.answer: Campus placements typically start in August-September for final year students. Pre-placement talks and aptitude tests begin from 7th semester.

qa_pairs.question: Which companies visit SRM for placements?
qa_pairs.answer: Top recruiters include TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Cognizant, Accenture, Amazon, Zoho, and 500+ more companies annually.

qa_pairs.question: What is the average placement package at SRM?
qa_pairs.answer: Average package is around ₹5 to 7 LPA. Highest packages go up to ₹40+ LPA from top tech companies.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a college bus facility?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, SRM operates buses from 50+ routes across Chennai and surrounding areas. Routes available at the Transport Office near the main gate.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get a bus pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for a bus pass at the Transport Office with your ID card and fee payment receipt. Annual pass costs ₹15,000 to ₹40,000 based on route.

qa_pairs.question: What time do college buses arrive and depart?
qa_pairs.answer: Morning buses arrive at campus between 7:30 to 8:00 AM. Evening buses depart at 5:15 PM and 6:00 PM. Check Transport Office for your route.

qa_pairs.question: What is the student portal link?
qa_pairs.answer: The official student portal is https://academia.srmist.edu.in. Use your SRM email ID and password to log in.

qa_pairs.question: How do I log in to ACADEMIA portal?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to https://academia.srmist.edu.in and log in with your SRM email and password set during admission.
answer_steps: Open https://academia.srmist.edu.in in your browser.
Enter your SRM email ID (e.g. ab1234@srmist.edu.in).
Enter the password set during admission.
Click Login. Use Forgot Password if needed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the helpdesk email?
qa_pairs.answer: Student helpdesk: helpdesk@srmist.edu.in. For academic issues: academics@srmist.edu.in. Response within 1 to 2 working days.

qa_pairs.question: What are the admin office hours?
qa_pairs.answer: Admin office is open Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Closed on weekends and public holidays.

qa_pairs.question: How do I replace my ID card?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply at the admin block with a written request and ₹200 fee. New card issued within 3 working days.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get a bonafide certificate?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply on ACADEMIA under Certificates then Bonafide. Ready within 3 working days. Collect from admin office or download if available.

qa_pairs.question: What are the library timings?
qa_pairs.answer: Library is open Monday to Saturday 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM. Sundays 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Digital library is 24/7 via e-resources portal.

qa_pairs.question: Is there Wi-Fi on campus?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, high-speed Wi-Fi is available across campus including classrooms, labs, hostels, and library. Use your SRM credentials to connect.

qa_pairs.question: How do I change my ACADEMIA password?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to ACADEMIA under Profile then Change Password. If forgotten, click Forgot Password and reset via your registered SRM email.

qa_pairs.question: What clubs and activities are available?
qa_pairs.answer: SRM has 100+ clubs including technical, cultural, sports, and social service clubs. Register via the Student Activities portal.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a medical facility on campus?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, SRM has a 24/7 medical centre with doctors and ambulance. SRM Medical College Hospital is adjacent for emergencies.

qa_pairs.question: How do I apply for leave?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for leave through ACADEMIA under Leave Application or submit a letter to your class advisor. Medical leaves require a doctor certificate.

qa_pairs.question: How do I raise a complaint or grievance?
qa_pairs.answer: Submit grievances via ACADEMIA under Grievance Portal or email grievance@srmist.edu.in. For urgent issues visit the Student Welfare Office.

qa_pairs.question: What is the dress code?
qa_pairs.answer: SRM follows a formal dress code. Boys: formal shirt and trousers. Girls: formal salwar or saree. ID card must be worn at all times.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get a no objection certificate?
qa_pairs.answer: Apply for NOC at admin office or through ACADEMIA under Certificates. State the purpose clearly. Issued within 3 to 5 working days.

qa_pairs.question: Can I pay my semester fee in instalments?
qa_pairs.answer: Instalment requests are considered case by case by the Finance Office. Submit a parent request letter, reason, and proposed payment schedule before the fee deadline. Approval is not automatic.
answer_steps: Write a request letter explaining why you need instalment payment.
Attach supporting documents and parent consent if asked.
Submit the request to the Finance Office before the due date.
Wait for written approval before assuming the instalment request is accepted.
aliases: Can I split my semester fee?
Can I pay fees in parts?

qa_pairs.question: My fee payment is not reflected. What should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Online fee payments may take up to 24 hours to reflect on ACADEMIA. Keep your transaction reference and receipt ready. If the status still does not update after one working day, contact the Finance Office with your register number and payment proof.
answer_steps: Download or save your payment receipt and transaction ID.
Wait up to 24 hours for the payment to sync with ACADEMIA.
Check Finance then Fee Receipt again on the portal.
If it still fails, email or visit the Finance Office with your register number, amount paid, and transaction details.
aliases: I paid fees but it is not showing
My semester fee status is pending

qa_pairs.question: How do I get a fee letter for an education loan?
qa_pairs.answer: Students can request a fee structure letter or fee estimate for education loans from the Finance Office. Carry your student ID and admission details. Some requests can also be raised through ACADEMIA under Certificates or Student Requests.
answer_steps: Visit the Finance Office or raise a certificate/request on ACADEMIA.
Mention that the document is for an education loan.
Provide your register number, programme, and semester details.
Collect the signed fee estimate or loan support letter once approved.
aliases: I need a bank letter for education loan
Can I get fee structure for loan processing?

qa_pairs.question: How do I renew my scholarship for the next year?
qa_pairs.answer: Scholarship renewal usually requires maintaining the prescribed CGPA, acceptable attendance, and no major disciplinary issues. Renewal notices are announced at the beginning of the academic year. Re-submit updated documents through the scholarship portal if asked.

qa_pairs.question: What is the malpractice policy during exams?
qa_pairs.answer: Using phones, written slips, smart devices, or any unfair means in an exam is treated as malpractice. The paper may be cancelled and the case can be forwarded for disciplinary action. Students must follow all instructions given by the invigilator and Examination Department.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I miss an end semester exam?
qa_pairs.answer: If you miss an End Semester Exam, inform your department and the Examination Department immediately, especially in case of medical or emergency reasons. Ordinary absence is usually treated as a fail for that paper. Eligible students may have to appear for the next supplementary or special exam as permitted by the university.
aliases: Can I write later if I am absent for end sem?

qa_pairs.question: How can I apply for a scribe for exams?
qa_pairs.answer: Students who require a scribe or extra support during exams should apply in advance through the Examination Department with relevant medical or disability documents. Approval should be obtained before the hall ticket period closes.
answer_steps: Collect the prescribed request format from the Examination Department or your department office.
Attach the necessary medical or disability certificate.
Submit the request several days before the exam schedule begins.
Wait for approval and keep a copy of the permission letter with you.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I lose my hall ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: If you lose your hall ticket, first try re-downloading it from ACADEMIA. If that is not possible, contact the Examination Department or your department office before the exam. Do not wait until the last minute.
answer_steps: Log in to ACADEMIA and try downloading the hall ticket again.
Print a fresh copy if the portal is still open.
If the portal access is closed or blocked, contact the Examination Department immediately.
Carry your student ID card for verification.

qa_pairs.question: How is project viva conducted?
qa_pairs.answer: Project viva is usually scheduled by the department near the end of the semester. Students are expected to submit the report, present their work, and demonstrate the project if required. Evaluation is done by faculty panels based on presentation, implementation, and understanding.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I check my exam seating arrangement?
qa_pairs.answer: Exam seating arrangements are usually published on the department notice board, exam notice board, or the student portal shortly before the exam. Check one day before the paper to avoid confusion on the exam day.

qa_pairs.question: Can I change my elective or add/drop a course?
qa_pairs.answer: Elective changes and add/drop requests are usually allowed only during the announced registration window at the start of the semester. Approval from your faculty advisor or HOD may be required. Late requests are generally not entertained.
answer_steps: Check whether the add/drop window is currently open.
Discuss the requested change with your faculty advisor or mentor.
Submit the change through ACADEMIA or the department form, if applicable.
Wait for the updated course registration to appear before attending the new class regularly.

qa_pairs.question: When can I meet my faculty advisor or mentor?
qa_pairs.answer: Faculty advisor and mentor meeting slots are usually shared by the department or class advisor. Students should use the announced counselling hour, mentor hour, or departmental appointment process. For urgent academic issues, contact the advisor through official email and request a meeting slot.

qa_pairs.question: How are missed classes compensated?
qa_pairs.answer: If classes are cancelled due to holidays, events, or faculty unavailability, departments usually arrange makeup classes, merged sessions, or special slots. Students should check ACADEMIA, class groups, or department notices for the revised schedule.

qa_pairs.question: When does course registration happen?
qa_pairs.answer: Course registration is usually completed before the start of each semester through ACADEMIA. Students should finish registration, elective selection, and fee payment within the announced window to avoid timetable or attendance issues later.

qa_pairs.question: Does medical leave reduce attendance shortage automatically?
qa_pairs.answer: Medical leave does not automatically remove attendance shortage in every case. Students must submit valid medical proof and follow the condonation or approval process specified by the department. Final approval depends on university rules and department verification.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my attendance is marked incorrectly?
qa_pairs.answer: If attendance is marked wrongly, contact the concerned faculty member as early as possible with proof such as lab records, assignment submission, OD approval, or class screenshots if applicable. Do not wait until the end of the semester to raise the issue.
answer_steps: Check the wrong entry in ACADEMIA and note the subject and date.
Contact the faculty member or class advisor within one or two working days.
Share any proof that supports your correction request.
Follow up until the corrected attendance is updated on the portal.

qa_pairs.question: How long does OD take to update in attendance?
qa_pairs.answer: OD approval usually takes a few working days to reflect in the attendance portal after the department verifies the request. Keep a copy of the approval letter or event confirmation until the portal shows the update.

qa_pairs.question: Why is my result marked as withheld?
qa_pairs.answer: A withheld result can happen due to pending fee dues, malpractice review, missing internal marks, project clearance issues, or document verification. Contact the Examination Department and your department office to identify the exact reason and clear it quickly.

qa_pairs.question: How do I correct my name or details on the mark sheet?
qa_pairs.answer: Report mark sheet name or detail corrections to the Examination Department with a copy of your student ID and supporting documents. Corrections should be requested as soon as the error is noticed.
answer_steps: Check the exact spelling or detail mismatch on the mark sheet.
Collect proof such as your student ID, Aadhaar, or admission record.
Submit a correction request to the Examination Department.
Follow up until the revised record is confirmed.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get my provisional certificate after graduation?
qa_pairs.answer: After completing all programme requirements, students can apply for a provisional certificate through the Examination Department or the certificates section in ACADEMIA if available. Processing usually starts after degree completion is verified.
aliases: When can I get my provisional degree certificate?
How do I apply for provisional certificate?

qa_pairs.question: How do I change my hostel room after allotment?
qa_pairs.answer: Room change requests are handled by the Hostel Office and are subject to availability, medical needs, or genuine roommate issues. Students should submit the request formally instead of changing rooms on their own.
answer_steps: Visit the Hostel Office and ask for the room change procedure.
State the reason clearly, such as medical need or serious room issue.
Submit the request with any supporting proof if required.
Wait for written approval before shifting rooms.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get a hostel outing or night-out pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Students should request outing or night-out permission through the hostel warden or the prescribed hostel system before leaving campus. Permission depends on hostel rules, parent intimation, and return timing.

qa_pairs.question: How do I raise a hostel maintenance complaint?
qa_pairs.answer: Maintenance complaints such as electrical, plumbing, furniture, or water issues should be reported to the hostel office, warden, or the maintenance complaint register/app used by your hostel. Mention the room number clearly so the request can be tracked.
answer_steps: Note the exact issue and your hostel room number.
Report it to the warden, hostel office, or hostel maintenance portal if available.
Keep the complaint number or entry reference if one is given.
Follow up with the hostel office if the issue is not resolved within a reasonable time.

qa_pairs.question: How do I report mess-related complaints?
qa_pairs.answer: Mess complaints about food quality, hygiene, menu, or billing should be raised with the mess manager, hostel warden, or hostel office. Use the official feedback or complaint channel if your hostel provides one.

qa_pairs.question: Can I attend placements if I have arrears?
qa_pairs.answer: Most companies prefer students with no active arrears at the time of applying. Some drives may allow limited history of arrears, but the exact rule depends on the company. Always check the company criteria published on the CDC portal before applying.

qa_pairs.question: Who reviews my resume before placement drives?
qa_pairs.answer: Resume reviews are typically handled through the CDC team, faculty coordinators, or placement mentors before major drives. Students should keep a one-page updated resume and revise it based on the feedback shared during placement training sessions.
answer_steps: Prepare your latest one-page resume in the recommended format.
Upload it to the CDC portal or share it with the assigned mentor if asked.
Attend resume review or pre-placement preparation sessions.
Update the final resume before applying to companies.

qa_pairs.question: Does SRM help students get internships?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, departments and the CDC often circulate internship opportunities, training programmes, and company openings for eligible students. Many internship activities begin from the middle semesters, but eligibility varies by company and department.

qa_pairs.question: What placement training is provided by the college?
qa_pairs.answer: Placement training usually includes aptitude, coding, communication, mock interviews, resume preparation, and company-specific preparation sessions. The exact schedule depends on your department and the CDC plan for that semester.

qa_pairs.question: What documents are required for placement registration?
qa_pairs.answer: Students are usually asked to keep a current resume, recent passport-size photo, mark sheets, government ID proof, and basic academic details ready for placement registration. Some companies may ask for additional documents such as consolidated marks or internship proof.

qa_pairs.question: Can I change my bus route after taking the transport pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Bus route changes are usually allowed only if seats are available on the requested route. Students should submit the request to the Transport Office early, especially at the start of the semester when seat allocation is being finalized.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I lose my bus pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Report a lost bus pass to the Transport Office as soon as possible. A duplicate pass may be issued after identity verification and payment of the prescribed duplicate-pass fee. Carry your student ID card until the replacement is issued.
answer_steps: Visit the Transport Office and report the lost pass.
Show your student ID card and route details.
Complete the duplicate pass request form if required.
Pay the duplicate-pass fee and collect the new pass.

qa_pairs.question: What is the format of my SRM student email ID?
qa_pairs.answer: Student email IDs usually follow the institutional SRM format assigned during admission or onboarding. Check your admission communication, ACADEMIA profile, or IT helpdesk message for the exact address issued to you.

qa_pairs.question: How do I update my bank account details with the college?
qa_pairs.answer: Bank detail updates for scholarships, refunds, or reimbursements are usually handled through the student portal or the concerned administrative office. Students should submit the account details carefully along with proof such as a cancelled cheque or passbook copy if requested.
answer_steps: Check whether the update can be made through ACADEMIA or a student request form.
Keep your bank proof ready, such as a passbook front page or cancelled cheque.
Submit the corrected account details to the concerned office.
Verify later that the updated account has been recorded correctly.

qa_pairs.question: Is counselling support available for students on campus?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, students can approach the counselling or student wellness support system for stress, emotional well-being, and personal concerns. If you need urgent support, contact the student welfare office or medical centre immediately and ask for the counselling contact.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to submit an anti-ragging form?
qa_pairs.answer: Anti-ragging declarations or affidavits are usually required during admission or the start of the academic year, especially for freshers. Follow the instructions shared by the admission team or student affairs office and submit the form before the announced deadline.

qa_pairs.question: What documents should freshers bring during reporting or admission?
qa_pairs.answer: Freshers are usually asked to bring admission offer copies, fee receipt, transfer certificate if required, community or category documents, photo ID, passport-size photos, and original academic certificates for verification. Always follow the latest reporting checklist shared by the admission office.

qa_pairs.question: How do I confirm my admission after paying the initial fee?
qa_pairs.answer: Admission confirmation is usually completed after fee payment, document verification, and the official update from the admission office. Keep your payment receipt and application number safe, and check your registered email or admission portal for the confirmation message.

qa_pairs.question: Can I request a department or branch transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: Department or branch transfer requests are generally considered only under specific academic and seat-availability conditions after the first year or during the announced transfer window. Final approval depends on university policy, eligibility, and available seats in the requested department.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to submit my lab record for practical exams?
qa_pairs.answer: Many departments require lab records, observation notes, or signed record notebooks to be completed before practical exams or lab viva. The exact rule depends on the department and course faculty, so students should confirm this well before the practical schedule.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get an internship NOC from the college?
qa_pairs.answer: Students usually need an internship NOC from the department or placement office before joining an external internship. Submit the internship offer details, organization name, duration, and any form provided by the company. Processing time depends on department approval.
answer_steps: Collect the internship offer letter or confirmation email.
Submit the NOC request to your department or placement coordinator.
Include company details, internship dates, and purpose of the NOC.
Collect the signed NOC before your reporting date.

qa_pairs.question: How do I check out of the hostel when I withdraw or complete the year?
qa_pairs.answer: Hostel checkout requires room inspection, clearance of mess or maintenance dues, return of room property if applicable, and completion of the hostel clearance form. Students should not leave without taking final clearance from the hostel office or warden.
answer_steps: Inform the hostel office or warden about your planned checkout date.
Clear any pending mess, hostel, or damage dues.
Get the room inspected and complete the hostel clearance form.
Collect your final clearance acknowledgement before leaving.

qa_pairs.question: How do alumni request transcripts or academic documents later?
qa_pairs.answer: Alumni can request transcripts or academic documents through the Examination Department by submitting the required form, identity proof, and the applicable fee. Processing time depends on document type and archival verification, so apply well in advance if you need it for visas or higher studies.

qa_pairs.question: When is the final degree certificate issued?
qa_pairs.answer: The final degree certificate is usually issued after convocation or the official degree-award process is completed. Until then, eligible students typically use their provisional certificate and transcript for most academic and employment purposes.
